summary: Russian forces are intensifying operations across multiple axes, with the Ministry of Defense reporting the liberation of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region, while completing the assault on Konstantinovka and isolating Krasny Lyman in the Donetsk region. Ukraine launched long-range strikes against Russian oil refineries, hitting the Slovyansky refinery in Krasnodar region (300 km from the front line) and a refinery in Yaroslavl region (700 km from the border), as Zelensky confirmed. Both sides are escalating strikes on rear infrastructure, with Russian forces targeting Ukrainian gas stations with Geranium drones and Ukrainian forces striking refineries deep inside Russian territory.
key_points: Russian Armed Forces liberated Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region, per Russian MoD. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in Nikolaev region with Geraniums, hitting depots of boats, unmanned craft, and personnel. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)
Ukrainian long-range strikes hit the Slovyansky oil refinery in Krasnodar region (300 km from front) and a refinery in Yaroslavl region (700 km from border). (Source: @UkraineNow/82237)
Russian forces destroyed two Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft on an airfield, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)
Russian forces captured 5 strongpoints and 59 buildings in the northwestern part of Krasny Lyman, per Russian MoD. (Source: @tass_agency/382746)

Multiple ballistic missile threats were issued for Kyiv from Bryansk region, with 26 launchers reported and at least 2 ballistic missiles already launched at the capital. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68120, @mon1tor_ua/68122, @mon1tor_ua/68141)
Russian MoD reports the Ukrainian Armed Forces' position in Donetsk region continues to deteriorate, with Russian troops moving closer to Kramatorsk and Slavyansk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214)
Zaporozhye was attacked resulting in 8 wounded including two children, with a 5-year-old boy in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces are intensifying operations across multiple axes, with the Ministry of Defense reporting the liberation of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region, while completing the assault on Konstantinovka and isolating Krasny Lyman in the Donetsk region. Ukraine launched long-range strikes against Russian oil refineries, hitting the Slovyansky refinery in Krasnodar region (300 km from the front line) and a refinery in Yaroslavl region (700 km from the border), as Zelensky confirmed. Both sides are escalating strikes on rear infrastructure, with Russian forces targeting Ukrainian gas stations with Geranium drones and Ukrainian forces striking refineries deep inside Russian territory.

points: Ukrainian long-range strikes targeted two Russian oil refineries: the Slovyansky refinery in Krasnodar region (300 km from the front line) and a refinery in Yaroslavl region (700 km from the border), as confirmed by President Zelensky. (Source: @UkraineNow/82237)

The Ukrainian Armed Forces' position in the Donetsk region continues to deteriorate according to Bloomberg, with Russian troops moving closer to Kramatorsk and Slavyansk. Military analyst Michael Kofman noted Ukraine's attacks on Russian rear areas have shifted media attention away from the ongoing fight for Donetsk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214)

Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in Nikolaev region using Geranium drones, destroying depots of boats and unmanned craft along with personnel. The Russian MoD released objective monitoring footage of the strike. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)
Ukraine's Ministry of Finance data indicates the country expects to receive at least $154 billion from Western allies by 2030 to sustain operations during the conflict. (Source: @tass_agency/382724)

In Dnipropetrovsk region, Russian forces attacked over 40 times with drones and artillery during the day, destroying three gas stations, setting a sports school on fire, killing one person and wounding another, and igniting fires in a residential building and wheat field. (Source: @UkraineNow/82233, @UkraineNow/82229)
Two Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft were destroyed on an airfield by Russian forces, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)

Zaporozhye suffered a Russian attack leaving 8 wounded, including two children, with a 5-year-old boy in serious condition, according to the Regional Military Administration. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244)
Russian forces liberated Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
Russian forces destroyed two Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft on an airfield, as reported by the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)
Russian forces captured 5 strongpoints and 59 buildings in the northwestern part of Krasny Lyman, according to the Russian MoD. (Source: @tass_agency/382746)

Russian forces are completing the assault on Konstantinovka and isolating Krasny Lyman in the Donetsk region, while concentrating forces for the upcoming battle for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)

Russian strikes on Ukrainian gas stations have intensified, using both medium-range drones and Geranium drones with more powerful warheads, leading to fuel shortages in frontline areas. The gas station network in Trostyanets has been completely destroyed, with stations in Kharkov and Zaporozhye also regularly attacked. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217, @ukr_leaks_eng/31213)

A state of emergency was introduced in Crimea and Sevastopol this week to enable faster recovery efforts following Ukrainian strikes. Ukraine's information-combat operation on the Crimean direction aims to gain a stronger negotiating position through military pressure on the peninsula. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217)

The traditional summer decline in Russian fuel production has been compounded by Ukrainian strikes on refineries, leading to regional restrictions on gasoline and diesel fuel sales across the country. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217)
Russian forces captured 5 strongpoints and 59 buildings in the northwestern part of Krasny Lyman, according to the Russian MoD, as they work to isolate the settlement. (Source: @tass_agency/382746)
Russian forces are completing the heavy, months-long assault on Konstantinovka in the Donetsk region, while isolating and encircling Krasny Lyman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)

Russian forces are leveling the front on the Slavyansk direction and concentrating forces for the upcoming battle for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration, from which increasing numbers of civilians are evacuating. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)
The Ukrainian Armed Forces' position in the Donetsk region continues to deteriorate, with Russian troops moving closer to Kramatorsk and Slavyansk, per Bloomberg. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KABs) on eastern Kharkiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/66638)
A Shahed-type drone was reported moving from Dnipropetrovsk region toward Kirovograd region, and another drone was tracked heading toward Kharkiv from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/66626)

Russian MoD accused Ukrainian forces in Konstantinovka of throwing anti-tank mines into a basement containing civilians; a pensioner was evacuated by the 4th Brigade of the Southern Grouping of Forces. (Source: @tass_agency/382731)

Multiple ballistic missile threats were issued for Kyiv from Bryansk region, with the number of launcher installations increased to 26 ballistic missiles. At least 2 ballistic missiles were already launched at the capital. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68120, @mon1tor_ua/68122, @mon1tor_ua/68141)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was declared, with warnings of elevated ballistic strike risk for Kyiv in the near term. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68141)

Shahed-type drones were tracked over western Chernihiv region heading toward Kyiv region (Vyshhorod district), and another reactive drone was reported in the Chernobyl Exclusion Zone in Kyiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/66626, @kpszsu/66639, @eRadarrua/98478)

A Shahed drone continued moving toward Bila Tserkva, and another drone changed course toward Obukhiv. The air alarm for Kyiv was eventually called off at 05:16 on June 28. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68103, @mon1tor_ua/68102, @eRadarrua/98472)

Drones were tracked heading toward Sumy from both north and south, with additional drones moving along the Sumy-Chernihiv border heading south and in Sumy district heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/66638, @kpszsu/66636)
Air-to-air missiles were reported heading toward Sumy region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98504)
A pair of drones was tracked from Kharkiv region past Krasnokutsk toward Poltava region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98508)
Russian forces liberated Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
Russian forces liberated Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region, per the Russian MoD. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in Nikolaev region with Geraniums, hitting depots of boats, unmanned craft, and personnel. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)

Zaporozhye was attacked resulting in 8 wounded including two children, with a 5-year-old boy in serious condition. KAB strikes were also reported in Zaporozhye region. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244, @kpszsu/66649)

Drones were tracked over southern Mykolaiv region heading north, over the border of Mykolaiv region heading toward Voznesensk, and heading toward Zaporozhye from the south. (Source: @kpszsu/66636, @kpszsu/66638)
Two reactive drones were tracked past Nikopol heading toward Sofiyivka. (Source: @eRadarrua/98492)

In Dnipropetrovsk region, Russian forces attacked over 40 times with drones and artillery, destroying three gas stations, setting a sports school on fire, killing one and wounding another. (Source: @UkraineNow/82233, @UkraineNow/82229)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KABs) on Zaporozhye region, Donetsk region, and Kharkiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/66649)
Russian forces destroyed two Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft on an airfield, according to the Russian MoD. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)

At least 2 ballistic missiles were launched at Kyiv, with a high threat of Iskander-M launches from Bryansk region and 26 launcher installations reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68120, @mon1tor_ua/68122, @mon1tor_ua/68141)

Multiple Shahed-type drones were tracked across northern and central Ukraine, including over Chernihiv region toward Kyiv region, in the Chernobyl Exclusion Zone, toward Bila Tserkva, toward Obukhiv, toward Kharkiv from the north, and from Dnipropetrovsk toward Kirovograd region. (Source: @kpszsu/66626, @kpszsu/66639, @eRadarrua/98478, @mon1tor_ua/68103, @mon1tor_ua/68102)

Drones were tracked over Mykolaiv region heading toward Voznesensk, over southern Mykolaiv region heading north, toward Zaporozhye from the south, past Nikopol toward Sofiyivka, from Kharkiv past Krasnokutsk toward Poltava, and toward Sumy from multiple directions. (Source: @kpszsu/66638, @kpszsu/66636, @eRadarrua/98492, @eRadarrua/98508)
Air-to-air missiles were reported heading toward Sumy region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98504)

Russian forces used Geranium drones to strike a Ukrainian training camp in Nikolaev region, and have increasingly employed them against Ukrainian gas station networks including in Trostyanets, Kharkov, and Zaporozhye. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222, @ukr_leaks_eng/31217)

key_developments: Russian forces liberated Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region, continuing territorial gains in the southern and eastern directions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)

Ukraine struck two Russian oil refineries: Slovyansky refinery in Krasnodar region (300 km from front) and a refinery in Yaroslavl region (700 km from border), demonstrating continued deep-strike capability. (Source: @UkraineNow/82237)
Russian forces destroyed two Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft on an airfield, degrading Ukraine's already limited fixed-wing combat aviation. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)

Multiple ballistic missile threats from Bryansk region targeted Kyiv, with 26 launchers reported and at least 2 missiles already launched at the capital. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68120, @mon1tor_ua/68122, @mon1tor_ua/68141)

Russian forces captured 5 strongpoints and 59 buildings in northwestern Krasny Lyman and are completing the assault on Konstantinovka, concentrating forces for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration. (Source: @tass_agency/382746, @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)

Russian strikes on Ukrainian gas stations using Geranium drones have caused fuel shortages in frontline areas, with the network in Trostyanets completely destroyed. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217, @ukr_leaks_eng/31213)

Dnipropetrovsk region suffered over 40 Russian drone and artillery attacks in one day, with three gas stations destroyed, a sports school on fire, one killed and one wounded. (Source: @UkraineNow/82233, @UkraineNow/82229)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is escalating significantly on both sides. Russia is conducting its main summer offensive phase in the Donetsk region, with forces concentrating for the decisive battle for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration while making territorial gains in Dnepropetrovsk and Zaporozhye regions. Ukraine is responding with deep strikes against Russian energy infrastructure, hitting refineries at 300-700 km range, while facing intensified Russian strikes on its fuel distribution networks and military airfields. The ballistic missile threat to Kyiv from Bryansk region with 26 launchers represents a significant escalation in strategic pressure on the capital. Russia's apparent lifting of self-imposed restrictions on strike intensity, combined with the destruction of Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft, suggests Moscow is seeking to degrade Ukraine's defensive and offensive capabilities ahead of the autumn negotiating season.
sections.regional_response: Finland's President Alexander Stubb stated in a Politico interview that neither sanctions nor Russian military losses will compel the Kremlin to end aggression, but that Moscow fears domestic unrest and growing public demand for peace makes a negotiated scenario somewhat more plausible. Poland's President Nawrocki saw his approval rating rise to 54.8% (up 8.4%) after stripping Zelensky of the Order of the White Eagle, marking the best result in IBRiS rating history. The EU is reportedly shifting its defense strategy from expensive platforms like tanks and next-generation aircraft toward mass production of cheaper strike systems including drones, missiles, and guided bombs, drawing lessons from Iran's long-range strike campaign. Ukraine's Ministry of Finance projects needing at least $154 billion from Western allies by 2030 to sustain its war effort.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Russian MoD's claim that Ukrainian forces in Konstantinovka threw anti-tank mines into a civilian basement, if verified, would constitute a potential war crime; the 4th Brigade of the Southern Grouping of Forces reportedly evacuated a pensioner from the city. A source indicated forthcoming positive news regarding the destruction of several aircraft of the Russian Aerospace Forces, though no specifics were provided. The Ukrainian Armed Forces' information-combat operation targeting Crimea aims to gain leverage in negotiations through military pressure on the peninsula, though analysts judge Ukraine lacks resources for anything beyond limited-objective actions. The negotiation track remains stalled, with Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov expressing frustration over what he characterized as deception during the Anchorage talks, which some Russian analysts view as another Minsk-style delaying mechanism.
